Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Marietta Padua Valledor [Probate Code Section 11700]
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Marietta Padua Valledor [Probate Code Section 11700]
Off Calendar Aug-24-2020 Continued To Aug-31-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Tony Tyre, Esq., counsel for Marietta Vallador, personal representative (appearing telephonically); Geronimo Amigable, counsel for Desiderio de la Cruz, objector (appearing by video conferencing from the Philippines).

At the first call, the audio connection with Mr. Amigable is problematic. The Court passes the matter.

10:35 a.m. At the second call, the audio connection remains problematic despite various attempts to connect with Mr. Amigable. The Court will draft an Order which will be e-mailed to counsel reciting the Court's tentative ruling to grant the Petition, setting a date for receipt of written objection for August 26, 2020, and a final hearing date of August 31, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Marietta Padua Valledor [Probate Code Section 11700]
Off Calendar Jul-27-2020 Continued To Aug-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tony Tyre appeared by telephone for Marietta Valledor (Petitioner).
Lydia Anchetta in Propria Persona.
Ron Amigable (Attorney from the Philippines) appeared by zoom.

Ms. Anchetta stated that she submitted a letter to the Court, but it was returned back to her and stated her position with respect to the original will being destroyed. Mr. Tyre stated that his Client made a significant effort to find the original will, that the copy of the will shows that Ms. Anchetta was removed, that the Decedent had an estranged relationship with Ms. Anchetta and that he does not believe Ms. Anchetta is a blood relative. Mr. Amigable stated that two of the Beneficiaries were not supposed to have been deleted from the will, that the initials were done improperly and there should have been full signatures of the Decedent, that he suspects someone else crossed their names, and that they were caregivers and were promised to receive from the estate. Mr. Tyre stated that they have a handwritten letter where the Decedent indicates that he had deleted names, that Mr. Amigable filed creditor's claims and that he believes his Client recently found the letter. The Court expressed concern about the late development, stated that it would like to see the letter, that the letter is to be provided to Counsel in the Philippines and made note of Article 7 of the will which shows Ms. Anchetta as someone who is intentionally omitted. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is continued to August 24, 2020 a 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Anchetta that she needs to file her objections and serve a copy to Mr. Tyre.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Marietta Padua Valledor [Probate Code Section 11700]
Off Calendar Jul-13-2020 Continued To Jul-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tony Tyre appeared by telephone for Marietta Valledor (Petitioner).

The Court noted that everything was in order and that no one has objected. The Court granted the Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

At the conclusion of the Court's morning calendar, Lydia Anchetta (Cousin of Decedent) made an appearance in Propria Persona.

Ms. Anchetta stated that the case reflects a lost will, that she is objecting to the lost will being admitted, that the copy of the will that was provided was not notarized, and that the initials where parties names were crossed out do not match the initials at the bottom of the will. The Probate Examiner informed Ms. Anchetta that the matter can be put back on calendar, but she needs to put her objections in writing and suggested that Ms. Anchetta hire a Lawyer to assist her. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is now continued to July 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Anchetta's objections are to be filed by July 20, 2020.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Schultz specially appearing for Tony Tyre for Petitioner

Mr. Schultz stated the decedent was a resident of San Francisco who passed away in the Philippines. The proposed executor, a cousin, found the copy of the Will in a briefcase with other important, legal documents, they made a search for the original but could not find it.

The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
